comments.body: Can you please provide sources for your answers. Right now your answer is an almost exact copy of another user's answer: stackoverflow.com/a/18055478/19032206

answers.body:
You should be using

getResourceAsStream(...);

when the resource is bundled as a jar/war or any other single file package for that matter.

Create the OutputStream with 

OutputStream os = this.getClass()
    .getClassLoader()
    .getResourceAsStream("/templates/" + quoteNumber + ".pdf");

You want to store the pdf inside the jar, which is different from when running in your local environment (say with Eclipse) where the OS filesystem is used.

Modify the code to

You can't write to class loader resources. You can only read the resources.

OutputStream outputStream = new FileOutputStream("/templates/"+quoteNumber+".pdf");

title: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: URI is not hierarchical (in deployed application)
body:
I have deployed my app to jar file. I want to generate and store PDF in resource folder of my application, Everything is working for me in development but when I deployed I got error in live application

following is my code

        URL url = this.getClass().getResource("/templates");
        parentDirectory = new File(new URI(url.toString()));
        OutputStream outputStream = new FileOutputStream(parentDirectory.toString()+"/"+quoteNumber+".pdf");

everything is working fine and created PDf file successfully when I run my application from Eclips but I deploye application WAR file in server I got this error

my error full log is following
